\"Geographic coordinates\",\n      \"description\": \"WGS84 latitude and longitude coordinates for a place or geographically located entity.\",\n      \"category\": \"space\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"coordinates\",\n        \"lat_lon\",\n        \"location\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"place\",\n        \"event\",\n        \"organization\",\n        \"source\",\n        \"media\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"WGS84\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\n          \"latitude\",\n          \"longitude\"\n        ],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"latitude\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n            \"minimum\": -90,\n            \"maximum\": 90\n          },\n          \"longitude\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n            \"minimum\": -180,\n            \"maximum\": 180\n          },\n          \"altitude_m\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\"\n          },\n          \"crs\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"const\": \"WGS84\"\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        {\n          \"latitude\": 37.33182,\n          \"longitude\": -122.03118,\n          \"crs\": \"WGS84\"\n        }\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"currency_value\",\n      \"label\": \"Currency value\",\n      \"description\": \"A monetary amount with an ISO 4217 currency code.\",\n      \"category\": \"money\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"price\",\n        \"cost\",\n        \"market_value\",\n        \"amount_money\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"claim\",\n        \"observation\",\n        \"source\",\n        \"project\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"ISO-4217\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\n          \"amount\",\n          \"currency\"\n        ],\n \"^[A-Z]{3}$\"\n          },\n          \"as_of\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"format\": \"date-time\"\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        {\n measurement\",\n      \"description\": \"A quantified physical or conceptual measurement expressed in SI-compatible units.\",\n      \"category\": \"quantity\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"measurement\",\n        \"quantity\",\n        \"metric_value\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"SI\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\n          \"value\",\n          \"unit\"\n        ],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"value\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\"\n          },\n          \"unit\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"quantity_kind\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"uncertainty\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\"\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        {\n          \"value\": 1.82,\n          \"unit\": \"m\",\n          \"quantity_kind\": \"height\"\n        }\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"external_identifier\",\n      \"label\": \"External identifier\",\n      \"description\": \"A typed identifier from an external authority or registry.\",\n      \"category\": \"reference\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"wikidata_id\",\n        \"doi\",\n        \"orcid\",\n        \"authority_id\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"none\",\n \"properties\": {\n          \"authority\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"value\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"url\": {\n \"epistemology\",\n      \"description\": \"A normalized level describing how uncertain a node, relationship, answer, or interpretation remains.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"certainty_level\",\n        \"unknown_level\",\n        \"confidence_gap\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"claim\",\n        \"question\",\n        \"hypothesis\",\n suggested merge.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"validation_status\",\n        \"curation_status\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"claim\",\n        \"question\",\n hypothesis, answer, or relationship.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"support_strength\",\n        \"evidence_quality\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"claim\",\n \"none\",\n          \"weak\",\n          \"moderate\",\n          \"strong\",\n          \"decisive\",\n          \"contested\"\n        ]\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        \"moderate\"\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"communication_model\",\n      \"label\": \"Communication model\",\n      \"category\": \"communication\",\n      \"description\": \"The participant topology of a \"query_response\",\n          \"publication\"\n        ]\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        \"query_response\"\n      ]\n    }\n  ]\n}\n",

      "content": "{\r\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",\r\n  \"$id\": \"https://xananode.org/schemas/xananode-relationship-types.schema.v0.5.0.json\",\n  \"title\": \"XanaNode Relationship Type Registry\",\r\n  \"description\": \"Canonical and extension relationship type registry for XanaNode-compatible knowledge substrates.\",\r\n  \"type\": \"object\",\r\n  \"additionalProperties\": false,\r\n  \"required\": [\r\n    \"version\",\r\n    \"relationship_types\"\r\n  ],\r\n  \"properties\": {\r\n    \"version\": {\r\n      \"type\": \"string\"\r\n    },\r\n    \"namespace\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"pattern\": \"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*$\"\n    },\n    \"media_conventions\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"description\": \"Portable projection asset path conventions for relationship type media.\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"relationship_type_asset_path_template\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Relative path template for relationship type projection media. Use {type} as the relationship type placeholder.\"\n        },\n        \"relationship_category_asset_path_template\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"Relative path template for relationship category projection media. Use {category} as the category placeholder.\"\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"relationship_types\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\r\n      \"items\": {\r\n        \"type\": \"object\",\r\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\r\n        \"required\": [\r\n          \"namespace\",\r\n          \"id\",\r\n          \"type\",\r\n          \"label\",\r\n          \"category\",\r\n          \"meaning\",\n          \"color\",\n          \"inverse_color\",\n          \"line_style\",\n          \"inverse_line_style\",\n          \"default_weight\",\n          \"default_visibility\"\n        ],\r\n        \"properties\": {\r\n          \"namespace\": {\r\n            \"type\": \"string\",\r\n            \"pattern\": \"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*$\"\r\n          },\r\n          \"id\": {\r\n            \"type\": \"string\",\r\n            \"pattern\": \"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\r\n          },\r\n          \"type\": {\r\n            \"type\": \"string\",\r\n            \"pattern\": \"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\r\n          },\r\n          \"label\": {\r\n            \"type\": \"string\"\r\n          },\r\n          \"category\": {\r\n            \"type\": \"string\",\r\n            \"anyOf\": [\r\n              {\r\n                \"enum\": [\r\n                  \"causation\",\r\n                  \"citation\",\r\n                  \"claim_structure\",\r\n                  \"comparison\",\r\n                  \"composition\",\r\n                  \"creation\",\r\n                  \"definition\",\r\n                  \"evidence\",\n                  \"explanation\",\n                  \"generic\",\n                  \"governance\",\n                  \"evolution\",\n                  \"historical\",\n                  \"identity\",\n                  \"influence\",\n                  \"inquiry\",\n                  \"knowledge_workflow\",\n                  \"lineage\",\n                  \"location\",\n                  \"participation\",\n                  \"reasoning\",\n                  \"research_workflow\",\n                  \"representation\",\n                  \"revision\",\n                  \"schema_mapping\",\n                  \"social\",\n                  \"temporal\",\n                  \"uncertainty\",\n                  \"discovery\",\n                  \"cognition\",\n                  \"attention\",\n                  \"communication\"\n                ]\n              },\n              {\r\n                \"pattern\": \"^[a-z][a-z0-9_:-]*$\"\r\n              }\r\n            ]\r\n          },\r\n          \"meaning\": {\r\n            \"type\": \"string\"\r\n          },\r\n          \"inverse\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"color\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\",\n            \"description\": \"Canonical projection color for this relationship type when rendered from source to target.\"\n          },\n          \"inverse_color\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\",\n            \"description\": \"Canonical projection color for the inverse reading of this relationship type.\"\n          },\n          \"line_style\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"enum\": [\n              \"solid\",\n              \"dashed\",\n              \"dotted\",\n              \"double\"\n            ],\n            \"description\": \"Canonical projection line style for this relationship type.\"\n          },\n       "content": "{\n  \"$schema\": \"./substrate-layout.schema.json\",\n  \"id\": \"xananode.substrate-layout\",\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"description\": \"The standard directory layout for a portable XanaNode substrate root.\",\n  \"root_contract\": \"A substrate root is the directory containing substrate.json. Tools should be able to validate, pack, mount, and project a substrate from this root without needing private application state.\",\n  \"required_paths\": [\n    {\n      \"path\": \"substrate.json\",\n      \"role\": \"substrate_manifest\",\n      \"required\": true,\n      \"kind\": \"file\",\n      \"format\": \"json\",\n      \"description\": \"The substrate manifest declaring id, name, namespace, version, repository, imports, sharing policy, and build/provenance metadata.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    }\n  ],\n  \"recognized_paths\": [\n    {\n      \"path\": \"nodes/*.json\",\n      \"role\": \"protocol_nodes\",\n      \"kind\": \"glob\",\n      \"format\": \"json\",\n      \"description\": \"Canonical protocol node records. A hand-built substrate may use this directory directly.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"nodes.json\",\n      \"role\": \"protocol_node_bundle\",\n      \"kind\": \"file\",\n      \"format\": \"json\",\n      \"description\": \"An alternate bundled protocol node artifact with a top-level nodes array.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"relationships.json\",\n      \"role\": \"protocol_relationships\",\n      \"kind\": \"file\",\n      \"format\": \"json\",\n      \"description\": \"Typed relationship records for the substrate.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"assets/**\",\n      \"role\": \"portable_media_and_source_assets\",\n      \"kind\": \"glob\",\n      \"description\": \"Images, PDFs, audio, video, source snapshots, projection media, documents, and other files carried by the substrate.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"schemas/*.json\",\n      \"role\": \"local_schema_extensions\",\n      \"kind\": \"glob\",\n      \"format\": \"json\",\n      \"description\": \"Namespaced node type, relationship type, property, layout, or validation extensions.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"reports/*.json\",\n      \"role\": \"reports\",\n      \"kind\": \"glob\",\n      \"format\": \"json\",\n      \"description\": \"Merge, compatibility, health, validation, duplicate-file, and intake reports.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"packs/*\",\n      \"role\": \"mounted_substrate_packs\",\n      \"kind\": \"glob\",\n      \"description\": \"Unpacked substrate packs mounted into this substrate for local federation.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"imports/*\",\n      \"role\": \"incoming_substrate_material\",\n      \"kind\": \"glob\",\n      \"description\": \"Dropped-in incoming packs or source material awaiting review, mounting, importing, or merge.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"author-profile.json\",\n      \"role\": \"default_author_profile\",\n      \"kind\": \"file\",\n      \"format\": \"json\",\n      \"description\": \"Portable default author profile for hand-built or minimal substrates.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \"ro-crate-metadata.json\",\n      \"role\": \"research_object_metadata\",\n      \"kind\": \"file\",\n      \"format\": \"json\",\n      \"description\": \"Optional RO-Crate metadata describing the substrate as a research object.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    }\n  ],\n  \"authoring_paths\": [\n    {\n      \"path\": \"content/nodes/*.md\",\n      \"role\": \"markdown_authoring_nodes\",\n      \"kind\": \"glob\",\n      \"format\": \"markdown_frontmatter\",\n      \"description\": \"Markdown authoring projection used by Core, Workspace, Studio, and Hugo workflows. Tools may generate protocol nodes from these files.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": true\n    },\n    {\n      \"path\": \".xananode/**\",\n      \"role\": \"workspace_metadata\",\n      \"kind\": \"glob\",\n      \"description\": \"Local workspace metadata such as preview settings, author lists, caches, and opened-source copies. Public exports may omit private caches but must preserve metadata needed for authored workspace round trips.\",\n      \"tools_must_preserve\": false\n    }\n  ],\n  \"archive_rules\": [\n    \"A .substrate archive should contain every recognized path unless sharing policy excludes it.\",\n    \"Tools must not include .git, node_modules, public renderer output, transient caches, or generated build directories unless explicitly requested.\",\n    \"Every local media/source file referenced by asset_path, file, source_snapshot, primary media resolution, or registry projection metadata must be included or reported as missing.\",\n    \"Duplicate files should be reported by content hash rather than silently duplicated without provenance.\",\n    \"A hand-built substrate that follows this layout should open in Core, Workspace, Studio, Hugo, or any compatible projection without private tool state.\"\n  ]\n}\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/schemas/xananode-substrate-layout.v0.1.0.json",
      "content_id": "sha256:0ef1a5307ba2e725c2ee4be1e3f4e7d40f8250695b38d1d9ef76b4801b02460b",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",

      }

    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-examples-federation-example-readme.md",
      "title": "Examples / Federation Example / README",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "The README for Examples / Federation Example.",
      "artifact_path": "examples/federation-example/README.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/federation-example/README.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Federation Example\n\nThis example shows how two independently authored substrates can be compared and partially merged without forcing a single source of truth.\n\n- `substrate-a/` models \"knowledge substrate\" as a concept.\n- `substrate-b/` models \"memory substrate\" as a similar concept.\n- `review-workspace/` shows the temporary mounted review state where both concepts stay visible together with their possible-match overlap.\n- each substrate records an outbound `possible_match` relationship to the other substrate's local concept.\n- `merge-report.json` records the result of a federation/merge pass, including the decision to preserve both local concepts instead of collapsing them.\n- `compatibility-report.json` records the practical compatibility level reached by the example.\n- `substrate-diff.json` shows the exact node and relationship additions that appear when the two substrates are analyzed together.\n\nThe merge report does not overwrite either substrate. It records a reviewable mapping, keeps both local nodes intact, and makes the overlap explicit enough for tools to compare, mount, inspect, or later merge with human approval. The review workspace exists so the example does not stop at prose about merge behavior; it also shows an openable temporary substrate where the overlap is already visible.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/examples/federation-example/README.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:2189af21476cb584b5115b722863a872ca3cee3645e28966d617cdb02747b603",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/federation-example/README.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:374ef31120c43c6748b888265d3213caaea8420668f76e850887bd401e7c7eb2",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-examples-federation-example-review-workspace-readme.md",
      "title": "Examples / Federation Example / Review Workspace / README",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "The README for Examples / Federation Example / Review Workspace.",
      "artifact_path": "examples/federation-example/review-workspace/README.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/federation-example/review-workspace/README.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Federation Review Workspace\n\nThis example is the temporary review view a XanaNode tool can expose after mounting both federation example substrates together.\n\n- It keeps `example.researcher_a:concept/knowledge-substrate` and `example.researcher_b:concept/memory-substrate` as separate nodes.\n- It makes their `possible_match` overlap visible instead of silently collapsing them.\n- It adds a short trail and review concept so the mounted result can be opened as a real substrate, not only read as reports.\n\nThis is not the only possible review projection. It is one valid example of the non-destructive merge state that sits between \"independently authored substrates\" and \"human-approved permanent merge.\"\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/examples/federation-example/review-workspace/README.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:c87b1eda9dae756819033542c0979ef41f1b54bd61f17340433b330d12c8228a",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/federation-example/review-workspace/README.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:c87b1eda9dae756819033542c0979ef41f1b54bd61f17340433b330d12c8228a",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-examples-federation-example-substrate-a-readme.md",
      "title": "Examples / Federation Example / Substrate A / README",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "The README for Examples / Federation Example / Substrate A.",
      "artifact_path": "examples/federation-example/substrate-a/README.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/federation-example/substrate-a/README.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",

    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-examples-minimal-substrate-nodes-readme.md",
      "title": "Examples / Minimal Substrate / Nodes / README",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",

      "summary": "The README for Examples / Minimal Substrate / Nodes.",

      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Nodes\n\nEach file in this directory is a node.\n\nEvery node in this example includes relationship references. This is intentional: the example should demonstrate a connected substrate, not just isolated nodes plus a trail.\n\nThe canonical relationship list lives in `../relationships.json`.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/examples/minimal-substrate/nodes/README.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:0df202afb3b63430208f540dfbe058aa491476a214441ceb6f9a380c42782897",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/minimal-substrate/nodes/README.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:0df202afb3b63430208f540dfbe058aa491476a214441ceb6f9a380c42782897",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",

    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-examples-minimal-substrate-readme.md",
      "title": "Examples / Minimal Substrate / README",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "The README for Examples / Minimal Substrate.",
      "artifact_path": "examples/minimal-substrate/README.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/minimal-substrate/README.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Minimal XanaNode Substrate Example\r\n\r\nThis is the smallest useful XanaNode substrate that still follows the core rule:\r\n\r\n> Nodes should not float as isolated facts. Concepts are built from claims, claims are supported by sources, and trails are curated routes through an already-connected graph.\r\n\r\nThis example uses only canonical XanaNode Core node types:\r\n\r\n- `source`\r\n- `concept`\r\n- `claim`\r\n- `person`\r\n- `essay`\r\n- `fragment`\r\n- `trail`\r\n\r\nIt also uses canonical relationship types:\r\n\r\n- `supports`\r\n- `discusses`\r\n- `related_to`\r\n- `defines`\r\n- `has_claim`\r\n- `starts_with`\r\n- `continues_to`\r\n- `features`\r\n- `created`\r\n- `derived_from`\r\n- `transcludes`\r\n\r\n## What this example demonstrates\r\n\r\nThe graph is intentionally tiny, but it is not just a bag of nodes.\r\n\r\n```text\r\nAs We May Think\r\n  supports\r\n    A knowledge substrate is structure, not just information\r\n      defines\r\n        Knowledge Substrate\r\n\r\nAs We May Think\r\n  discusses\r\n    Associative Trails\r\n      related_to\r\n        Knowledge Substrate\r\n\r\nStart Here\r\n  starts_with\r\n    As We May Think\r\n\r\nRelationship Navigation Intro\r\n  transcludes\r\n    As We May Think Fragment 0004\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Why the relationships matter\r\n\r\nA normal hyperlink can show that the source, claim, and concept are somehow related.\r\n\r\nXanaNode requires the relationship to say **how** they are related:\r\n\r\n- The source `supports` the claim.\r\n- The claim `defines` the concept.\r\n- The concept `has_claim` so it is not unsupported.\r\n- The source `discusses` associative trails.\r\n- Associative trails are `related_to` the knowledge substrate as historical and conceptual context.\r\n- The trail provides a human reading route, but it is not the only connection.\r\n- The essay `transcludes` a fragment using the versioned tumbler address `example.minimal:source/as-we-may-think@git:8e47e70:examples/minimal-substrate/nodes/source-as-we-may-think.json#fragment/0004@sha256:example-as-we-may-think-fragment-0004`.\n\r\n## Files\r\n\r\n- `substrate.json` declares the substrate namespace and schema version.\r\n- `relationships.json` declares canonical typed edges.\r\n- `nodes/*.json` are individually addressable nodes with relationship references.\r\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/examples/minimal-substrate/README.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:f79ef045ea95cdee1511be66dca29b3ae6b3d944b98f3d9fb6645e8c9216a6b8",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/minimal-substrate/README.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:f79ef045ea95cdee1511be66dca29b3ae6b3d944b98f3d9fb6645e8c9216a6b8",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-examples-readme.md",
      "title": "Examples / README",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "The README for Examples.",
      "artifact_path": "examples/README.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/README.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# XanaNode Examples\n\nThese examples are protocol examples, not full applications.\n\nThey show how a substrate can declare a namespace, define nodes, create typed relationships, and produce a federation merge report.\n\n## Examples\n\n- `minimal-substrate/` - the smallest useful XanaNode substrate.\n- `federation-example/` - two independently authored substrates plus a merge report.\n\nThe examples intentionally use JSON files instead of a specific CMS, static-site generator, database, or graph renderer. Implementations may store the same structures in Markdown front matter, a database, RDF, graph stores, or other formats.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/examples/README.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:acf9a69904b1b6a8d1ed3e301347ee4f7f261c5daee9dfc01628a6cc5a08a76d",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/examples/README.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:06ecf825c7fb006ddd5df353c152e3c00aa0c47bb498ecee1f60f11068139a80",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-governance-canonical-type-policy.md",
      "title": "Governance / Canonical Type Policy",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A governance document covering Canonical Type Policy.",
      "artifact_path": "governance/canonical-type-policy.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/canonical-type-policy.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Canonical Type Policy\n\nCanonical types are the shared vocabulary that make independent XanaNode substrates interoperable.\n\nA type should become canonical only when it is:\n\n- broadly useful across many domains\n- clearly distinguishable from existing types\n- describable in plain language\n- stable enough to validate\n- useful to both humans and machines\n\nAvoid adding canonical types for every domain-specific object. Prefer subtypes or namespaced extensions.\n\n## Projection Style Governance\n\nCanonical relationship types include visual projection metadata. Every canonical relationship type must define:\n\n- `color`\n- `inverse_color`\n- `line_style`\n- `inverse_line_style`\n\nThese values are part of the shared vocabulary. Renderers use them to stratify connective lines so relationship meaning remains visible in graph projections, trails, maps, and other views.\n\nImplementations may adapt the exact rendered appearance for accessibility, contrast, print, color-blind themes, or device constraints, but they should not collapse all relationship types into one undifferentiated line treatment while claiming full protocol projection compliance.\n\n## Multi-Role Node Projection\n\nThe primary node `type` remains the authoritative validation and routing category. Secondary roles belong in `facets`, and narrower labels belong in `subtype` or `subtypes`.\n\nProjection layers should preserve visible evidence of every recognized role. When a node has facets that match known node types, the projected mark should mix the primary type color with the facet type colors. Slices, rings, bands, layered borders, or another accessible mixed treatment are all valid. The important governance rule is that the projection must not silently flatten a multi-role node into one color when the substrate says the node participates in several modeling categories.\n\nSchema vocabulary terms, schema fields, property registry entries, relationship categories, subtype entries, governance rules, and projection style rules should be modeled as `schema` nodes with specific subtypes. Those terms are part of the substrate vocabulary, not private implementation comments.\n\n## Canonical vs Extension\n\nUse canonical types for common substrate structure.\n\nUse extensions for domain-specific modeling.\n\nExample:\n\n- `xananode:media` should be canonical.\n- `museum:artifact` should be an extension.\n- `biology:species` should be an extension unless future adoption proves otherwise.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/governance/canonical-type-policy.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:52aec3764ac7edef089f6dbd4782dd902dce04e2c5d388d5fceaaa62c24ad9ad",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/canonical-type-policy.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:52aec3764ac7edef089f6dbd4782dd902dce04e2c5d388d5fceaaa62c24ad9ad",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-governance-dispute-resolution.md",
      "title": "Governance / Dispute Resolution",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A governance document covering Dispute Resolution.",
      "artifact_path": "governance/dispute-resolution.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/dispute-resolution.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Dispute Resolution\n\nXanaNode treats disputes as knowledge, not merely failure.\n\nA disputed claim should remain visible when appropriate, along with:\n\n- who disputes it\n- what evidence supports it\n- what counterevidence challenges it\n- what assumptions differ\n- what sources are involved\n\nDisputes can be represented with relationships such as:\n\n- `contradicts`\n- `contradicted_by`\n- `disputes`\n- `disputed_by`\n- `alternative_to`\n- `possible_match`\n- `disputed_identity`\n\nThe goal is not instant consensus. The goal is navigable uncertainty.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/governance/dispute-resolution.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:ce5f64b3bd7d9d47b1314a3431aa0d9195482f3c73a3ed87c32f0b6b2d6bce2d",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/dispute-resolution.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:ce5f64b3bd7d9d47b1314a3431aa0d9195482f3c73a3ed87c32f0b6b2d6bce2d",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-governance-federation-rules.md",
      "title": "Governance / Federation Rules",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A governance document covering Federation Rules.",
      "artifact_path": "governance/federation-rules.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/federation-rules.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Federation Rules\n\nFederation connects independently authored substrates without forcing one source of truth.\n\nA federation process should:\n\n1. Load each substrate manifest.\n2. Resolve each substrate's Git repository metadata.\n3. Validate each substrate against declared schemas.\n4. Preserve original node IDs and namespaces.\n5. Detect likely overlaps.\n6. Generate mapping relationships.\n7. Preserve conflicts and uncertainty.\n8. Avoid destructive merging.\n\nFederation should create more knowledge, not erase local context.\n\n## Never Collapse by Default\n\nTwo nodes that look similar should not automatically become one node.\n\nPrefer:\n\n- `same_entity`\n- `possible_match`\n- `disputed_identity`\n- `equivalent_to`\n\ndepending on confidence and review status.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/governance/federation-rules.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:a9f33cdd2a8eea27097f01a0dedfbb1659a81c2f3bb4094053f3544cd703fd23",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/federation-rules.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:a9f33cdd2a8eea27097f01a0dedfbb1659a81c2f3bb4094053f3544cd703fd23",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-governance-moderation-model.md",
      "title": "Governance / Moderation Model",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A governance document covering Moderation Model.",
      "artifact_path": "governance/moderation-model.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/moderation-model.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Moderation Model\n\nXanaNode does not require one universal moderation authority.\n\nEach substrate controls its own moderation rules.\n\nFederation allows independently moderated substrates to be analyzed together while preserving their local decisions.\n\nA substrate may choose to:\n\n- approve nodes\n- reject nodes\n- deprecate claims\n- preserve disputed claims\n- hide low-confidence relationships\n- publish merge reports\n\nModeration actions should themselves be traceable whenever possible.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/governance/moderation-model.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:ed7db2a6ee2c8ab204059c8835ebcff1d326372271771931af8c684691a13bb2",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/moderation-model.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:ed7db2a6ee2c8ab204059c8835ebcff1d326372271771931af8c684691a13bb2",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-governance-readme.md",
      "title": "Governance / README",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A governance document covering README.",
      "artifact_path": "governance/README.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/README.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# XanaNode Governance\n\nXanaNode governance defines how the protocol evolves while allowing independent substrates to remain sovereign.\n\nThe governance model is intentionally minimal:\n\n1. Maintain a small stable core.\n2. Allow namespaced extensions.\n3. Preserve disagreement rather than deleting it.\n4. Treat mappings, disputes, and merge results as knowledge.\n5. Avoid centralizing truth in one moderation team.\n\nGovernance applies to the protocol repository. It does not dictate how every independent substrate must moderate its own content.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/governance/README.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:00b02bc523d88ae79002e06dd68847fb67cdd3f05a94b775edfe7c67cbb64bc5",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/README.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:00b02bc523d88ae79002e06dd68847fb67cdd3f05a94b775edfe7c67cbb64bc5",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-governance-schema-extension-rules.md",
      "title": "Governance / Schema Extension Rules",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A governance document covering Schema Extension Rules.",
      "artifact_path": "governance/schema-extension-rules.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/schema-extension-rules.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Schema Extension Rules\n\nSubstrates may define custom node and relationship types.\n\nCustom types must be:\n\n- namespaced\n- versioned\n- human-readable\n- machine-readable\n- preserved even when not understood\n- optionally mapped to a core type\n\nA valid extension should include:\n\n- `id`\n- `namespace`\n- `type`\n- `label`\n- `purpose` or `meaning`\n- `core: false`\n- optional `extends`\n\nUnknown extension types should not break import. They should be marked opaque and preserved.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/governance/schema-extension-rules.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:faf22ba3636ce404d1ceee0e1829c44a87a9b44859eb00bbaec4c0935ee565ab",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/schema-extension-rules.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:faf22ba3636ce404d1ceee0e1829c44a87a9b44859eb00bbaec4c0935ee565ab",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-governance-versioning-policy.md",
      "title": "Governance / Versioning Policy",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A governance document covering Versioning Policy.",
      "artifact_path": "governance/versioning-policy.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/versioning-policy.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# Versioning Policy\n\nXanaNode protocol files use semantic versioning where practical.\n\n- Patch versions fix wording or validation mistakes.\n- Minor versions add compatible types, fields, or examples.\n- Major versions change compatibility expectations.\n\nSchemas should be versioned independently from individual substrates.\n\nSubstrates should declare which core schema version they target.\n\nSubstrate history should be tracked in Git. Protocol version fields describe schema compatibility; Git commits describe concrete substrate revisions.\n\nExample:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"schema_version\": \"xananode-core@0.5.0\"\n}\n```\n\nBreaking changes should go through the proposal process.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/governance/versioning-policy.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:64cb4a5db286236b931f23c09d99ea99e00319efea5e9dbe4cd850c1f331eb96",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/governance/versioning-policy.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:64cb4a5db286236b931f23c09d99ea99e00319efea5e9dbe4cd850c1f331eb96",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },

      "content": "# XanaNode Protocol Licensing\n\nXanaNode uses a split license model.\n\n## Documentation\n\nProtocol documentation, explanatory text, specifications, governance text, proposals, examples, and README files are licensed under:\n\n`CC-BY-4.0`\n\nYou may share and adapt the documentation with attribution.\n\n## Schemas, Validators, And Reference Code\n\nJSON Schemas, schema registries, validators, tooling, examples intended as machine-readable fixtures, and reference implementation code are licensed under:\n\n`Apache-2.0`\n\nYou may use, modify, distribute, and implement these artifacts under the Apache License 2.0.\n\n## Trademarks\n\nThe XanaNode name, XanaNode logo, and related brand assets are trademarks or trade dress of the XanaNode project. License grants for documentation, schemas, validators, and code do not grant trademark rights.\n\nSee [TRADEMARK.md](TRADEMARK.md).\n\n",

    {

      "content": "# XanaNode Proposals\n\nXanaNode Proposals, or XNPs, are design records for protocol changes.\n\nUse proposals for changes that affect:\n\n- canonical schemas\n- federation behavior\n- namespace rules\n- compatibility levels\n- transclusion\n- tumbler addressing\n- Git-backed substrates\n- derived inverse relationships\n- standards alignment profiles\n- federation discovery and trust\n- Studio local-first authoring\n- governance\n\nProposal statuses:\n\n- Draft\n- Review\n- Accepted\n- Rejected\n- Superseded\n- Experimental\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/README.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:2247432bc955a1cb08c44001def4fe58ec5889aabe10da14f80902cd3a9a8e5a",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/proposals/README.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:2247432bc955a1cb08c44001def4fe58ec5889aabe10da14f80902cd3a9a8e5a",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }

    {

      "content": "# XNP-0001: Namespaces\n\nStatus: Accepted\n\n## Summary\n\nDefines how substrates and extension schemas avoid ID collisions.\n\n## Motivation\n\nXanaNode aims to support independently authored knowledge substrates that can later interoperate. This proposal records the design expectations for namespaces so implementations can converge without requiring centralized control.\n\n## Requirements\n\n- Must remain human-readable.\n- Must remain machine-interpretable.\n- Must preserve provenance.\n- Must avoid destructive merging.\n- Must allow independent substrate ownership.\n\n## Open Questions\n\n- What is the smallest useful implementation?\n- Which parts are required for compatibility?\n- Which parts should remain optional or experimental?\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/XNP-0001-namespaces.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:7412974fb68c130d03039ab1a1c6977dc326c910411b6b596a9ac58044d3c327",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",

        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:7412974fb68c130d03039ab1a1c6977dc326c910411b6b596a9ac58044d3c327",

      "content": "# XNP-0005: Transclusion\r\n\r\nStatus: Experimental\r\n\r\n## Summary\r\n\r\nDefines inclusion by reference rather than copying.\r\n\r\nThe initial interoperable form uses a `transcludes` relationship from a consuming node to a fragment node with a stable `tumbler` address.\r\n\r\n## Motivation\r\n\r\nXanaNode aims to support independently authored knowledge substrates that can later interoperate. This proposal records the design expectations for transclusion so implementations can converge without requiring centralized control.\r\n\r\n## Requirements\r\n\r\n- Must remain human-readable.\r\n- Must remain machine-interpretable.\r\n- Must preserve provenance.\r\n- Must avoid destructive merging.\r\n- Must allow independent substrate ownership.\r\n\r\n## Open Questions\r\n\r\n- What is the smallest useful implementation?\r\n- Which parts are required for compatibility?\r\n- Which parts should remain optional or experimental?\r\n",

      "content": "# XNP-0006: Tumbler Addressing\r\n\r\nStatus: Experimental\r\n\r\n## Summary\r\n\r\nDefines persistent addressing for nodes and fragments.\r\n\r\nThe initial accepted profile is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n<namespace>:<node-kind>/<local-id>\n<namespace>:<node-kind>/<local-id>@<version-id>\n<namespace>:<node-kind>/<local-id>@<source-version-id>#fragment/<fragment-id>@<fragment-version-id>\n```\n\r\n## Motivation\r\n\r\nXanaNode aims to support independently authored knowledge substrates that can later interoperate. This proposal records the design expectations for tumbler addressing so implementations can converge without requiring centralized control.\r\n\r\n## Requirements\r\n\r\n- Must remain human-readable.\r\n- Must remain machine-interpretable.\r\n- Must preserve provenance.\r\n- Must avoid destructive merging.\r\n- Must allow independent substrate ownership.\r\n\r\n## Open Questions\r\n\r\n- What is the smallest useful implementation?\r\n- Which parts are required for compatibility?\r\n- Which parts should remain optional or experimental?\r\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/XNP-0006-tumbler-addressing.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:a8625f3aae9404ea63bf5474f066ffc532b9166afb38462c03a7f27b8a5eed82",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",

    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-proposals-xnp-0007-git-backed-substrates.md",
      "title": "Proposals / XNP 0007 Git Backed Substrates",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A XanaNode proposal covering XNP 0007 Git Backed Substrates.",
      "artifact_path": "proposals/XNP-0007-git-backed-substrates.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/proposals/XNP-0007-git-backed-substrates.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# XNP-0007: Git-Backed Substrates\n\nStatus: Accepted\n\n## Summary\n\nDefines Git as the required versioning and history layer for production XanaNode substrates.\n\n## Decision\n\nEach production substrate is a Git repository. The substrate manifest must include a `repository` block describing the Git remote and default branch.\n\nGit provides:\n\n- revision history\n- branching\n- review\n- merge\n- synchronization\n- rollback\n\nXanaNode provides:\n\n- node and relationship artifacts\n- schema validation\n- provenance fields\n- merge and compatibility reports\n- federation semantics\n\n## Rationale\n\nXanaNode should not reinvent distributed version control. Git already provides the substrate-level history model that knowledge authors need, while XanaNode adds knowledge-specific structure on top.\n\nUser-facing tools may rename Git concepts for approachability, such as \"save snapshot\" for commit or \"draft path\" for branch, but the underlying substrate history remains Git.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/XNP-0007-git-backed-substrates.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:b24c29fbe0a89e6b6d6ba67997a6e674e4fe5ba2811bdf4f858255aedb295c6d",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/proposals/XNP-0007-git-backed-substrates.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:b24c29fbe0a89e6b6d6ba67997a6e674e4fe5ba2811bdf4f858255aedb295c6d",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-proposals-xnp-0008-derived-inverse-relationships.md",
      "title": "Proposals / XNP 0008 Derived Inverse Relationships",
      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A XanaNode proposal covering XNP 0008 Derived Inverse Relationships.",
      "artifact_path": "proposals/XNP-0008-derived-inverse-relationships.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/proposals/XNP-0008-derived-inverse-relationships.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# XNP-0008: Derived Inverse Relationships\n\nStatus: Accepted\n\n## Summary\n\nDefines inverse relationship labels as derived views rather than separate canonical relationship types.\n\n## Decision\n\nCanonical relationship types are authored in one direction only. A relationship type may declare an `inverse` label for display, querying, or generated indexes, but the inverse should not be registered as a second canonical type.\n\nExample:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"type\": \"created\",\n  \"inverse\": \"created_by\"\n}\n```\n\nA relationship record should store:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"source\": \"example:person/alice\",\n  \"target\": \"example:essay/intro\",\n  \"type\": \"created\"\n}\n```\n\nAn implementation may display `created_by` when viewing the target node, but should not store a second `created_by` relationship type or edge.\n\n## Rationale\n\nRegistering both directions bloats the canonical vocabulary and creates avoidable consistency risks:\n\n- duplicate relationship type definitions\n- mismatched inverse pairs\n- dangling inverse types\n- unnecessary authoring choices\n- harder validation\n\nDerived inverse views keep the graph navigable in both directions without doubling the protocol vocabulary.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/XNP-0008-derived-inverse-relationships.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:acc4170400d597124486a22325d0f5e31c16d5f39f536a9265dbefc4783d262f",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/proposals/XNP-0008-derived-inverse-relationships.md",
        "method": "archive",
        "content_id": "sha256:acc4170400d597124486a22325d0f5e31c16d5f39f536a9265dbefc4783d262f",
        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
    },

      "content": "# XNP-0009: Standards Alignment Profiles\n\nStatus: Accepted\n\n## Summary\n\nDefines optional interoperability profiles for JSON-LD, Web Annotation selectors, PROV-O, content IDs, RO-Crate, and nanopublication-style claim packages.\n\n## Decision\n\nXanaNode keeps its native artifacts simple, but provides mappings and companion profiles for standards-aware tools.\n\nAccepted profiles:\n\n- JSON-LD context\n- Web Annotation selector names\n- PROV-O provenance mapping\n- optional content IDs and version IDs\n- RO-Crate-compatible preservation metadata\n- nanopublication-like claim packaging\n\nThese profiles are optional for basic substrates but recommended for archival, scholarly, legal, scientific, and federated deployments.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/XNP-0009-standards-alignment.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:c7e019264ebe4e77fee11f412a2e13a7c55315be5987025ff0c4ddacebf67b40",

      "content": "# XNP-0010: Federation Discovery and Trust\n\nStatus: Accepted\n\n## Summary\n\nDefines optional discovery and trust metadata in substrate manifests.\n\n## Decision\n\nSubstrate manifests may include:\n\n- federation inbox\n- federation outbox\n- subscription or feed URL\n- merge report endpoint\n- mapping endpoint\n- signature metadata\n- public key metadata\n- trust scopes\n\nTrust scopes are:\n\n- `local_substrate`\n- `imported_schema`\n- `mapping`\n- `assertion`\n\n## Rationale\n\nGit-backed substrates can federate through repository exchange, but active federation benefits from discoverable update and mapping endpoints. Signatures and public keys provide a path toward verifiable manifests, merge reports, mappings, and assertions.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/XNP-0010-federation-discovery-and-trust.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:7cdebf803484c1de623f59f7a931dd35d7a569c259bfe601bac65e70c6ebe2de",

      "content": "# XNP-0011: Studio Local-First Authoring\n\nStatus: Draft\n\n## Summary\n\nCaptures the Studio product direction as local-first Git-backed authoring for knowledge substrates.\n\n## Decision\n\nStudio should treat a substrate as a local Git repository and expose user-facing concepts before raw Git mechanics.\n\nAuthor-facing language:\n\n- save snapshot\n- draft path\n- bring changes together\n- propose changes\n- what changed\n- history\n\nThe Studio MVP should focus on opening substrate repos, editing nodes, adding relationships, validating, previewing with the Hugo implementation, saving snapshots, and publishing.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/XNP-0011-studio-local-first-authoring.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:3cc4f574705261f5fcaf53eeb0152d530385a3cf13c4a7030edabb4cbc01a40e",

      "id": "xananode.canonical:source/protocol-artifact-proposals-xnp-0012-executable-tool-contract.md",

      "type": "source",
      "subtype": "protocol_document",
      "importance": 3,
      "summary": "A XanaNode proposal covering XNP 0012 Executable Tool Contract.",
      "artifact_path": "proposals/XNP-0012-executable-tool-contract.md",
      "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/proposals/XNP-0012-executable-tool-contract.md",
      "media_type": "document",
      "mime_type": "text/markdown",
      "asset_role": "canonical_raw_source",
      "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
      "relationships": [],
      "content": "# XNP-0012: Executable Tool Contract\n\n## Status\n\nDraft\n\n## Summary\n\nDefine a stable executable contract for official XanaNode tooling so applications can call Core and Workspace behavior without embedding Node.js directly.\n\n## Motivation\n\nXanaNode is a protocol, not a JavaScript framework.\n\nRight now, the reference stack is implemented in Node-based repositories:\n\n- XanaNode-Protocol\n- XanaNode-Core-SDK\n- XanaNode-Workspace\n- XanaNode-Hugo\n- XanaNode-Studio\n- XanaNode-Mobile\n\nThat is acceptable as an implementation phase, but it is not the right long-term dependency story for the protocol itself.\n\nIf a desktop app, mobile app, static-site builder, AI agent, or institutional integration must install Node and call private JavaScript modules directly, then the protocol boundary is too soft. The protocol should be callable from any stack that can execute a program, pass files, and read JSON results.\n\n## Proposal\n\nOfficial tooling should converge on two primary executable surfaces:\n\n- `xananode-core`\n- `xananode-workspace`\n\nThese names describe roles, not implementation language. They may be delivered as:\n\n- native binaries\n- packaged runtime executables\n- OS-specific launchers\n- language wrappers that preserve the same command contract\n\n## Goals\n\n1. Keep protocol behavior callable from any language or host environment.\n2. Separate implementation language from protocol contract.\n3. Give Studio, Mobile, Hugo helpers, AI agents, and third-party tools one stable thing to call.\n4. Keep artifact generation, intake analysis, and projection derivation aligned across the stack.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n1. This proposal does not replace language SDKs.\n2. This proposal does not forbid direct in-process library use.\n3. This proposal does not require one operating-system packaging strategy.\n\n## Core responsibilities\n\n`xananode-core` should own:\n\n- substrate validation\n- protocol inspection\n- artifact builds\n- portable bundle generation\n- fragment derivation\n- review suggestion generation\n- intake analysis\n- projection-data derivation\n\nRecommended commands:\n\n- `validate`\n- `inspect`\n- `build`\n- `bundle`\n- `analyze-intake`\n- `projection`\n\n## Workspace responsibilities\n\n`xananode-workspace` should own:\n\n- substrate initialization\n- workspace open/create behavior\n- author and repository enforcement\n- local build orchestration\n- portable export\n- mounted substrate intake\n- working-copy creation\n- de-intertwingle cleanup\n- health reporting\n- snapshot orchestration\n\nRecommended commands:\n\n- `init`\n- `open`\n- `build`\n- `export`\n- `intertwingle`\n- `deintertwingle`\n- `health`\n- `snapshot`\n\n## Input and output rules\n\nExecutable implementations should:\n\n- accept filesystem paths to substrate folders, `.substrate` archives, `substrate-bundle.json`, and `substrate-bundle.jsonl`\n- support structured JSON output for machine callers\n- emit non-zero exit codes on failure\n- expose build metadata including implementation version and commit when possible\n- preserve protocol semantics regardless of implementation language\n\nMachine callers should not be expected to scrape prose logs to determine success.\n\n## Why this matters\n\nXanaNode is supposed to let knowledge move across tools and media without losing structure.\n\nThe same principle applies to the tooling itself.\n\nIf the protocol is real, then:\n\n- a Kotlin Android app should be able to call it\n- a Rust CLI should be able to call it\n- a Python workflow should be able to call it\n- a static-site renderer should be able to call it\n- an AI agent should be able to call it\n\nwithout pretending JavaScript is the protocol.\n\n## Transition path\n\n1. Keep the current Node reference implementations.\n2. Tighten their command contracts and JSON outputs.\n3. Package them as self-contained executables for supported platforms.\n4. Teach official tools to prefer the executable boundary.\n5. Allow alternate implementations later as long as they preserve the same artifact rules and command semantics.\n\n## Compatibility\n\nThis proposal is additive.\n\nExisting repositories may continue to expose:\n\n- Node modules\n- npm CLIs\n- internal helper APIs\n\nBut official documentation should increasingly describe the executable contract as the stable cross-stack surface.\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/proposals/XNP-0012-executable-tool-contract.md",
      "content_id": "sha256:e7645ce8343d094dd4d5138f31b237e2160f111bfe0f9f7fd6ebcb4f6e4637e7",
      "source_snapshot": {
        "captured_at": "2026-06-27T16:35:32.999Z",
        "source_url": "https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Protocol/blob/main/proposals/XNP-0012-executable-tool-contract.md",

        "rights_status": "CC-BY-4.0",
        "tool": "@xananode/core"
      }
